words.def: the state of feeling nervous or worried that something bad is going to happen
words.example: acute/intense/deep anxiety

words.def: feeling worried or nervous
words.example: The bus was late and Sue began to get anxious.

words.def: a word or statement saying sorry for something that has been done wrong or that causes a problem
words.example: to offer/make/demand/accept an apology

words.def: easy to see or understand
words.example: Their devotion was apparent.

words.def: according to what you have heard or read; according to the way something appears
words.example: Apparently they are getting divorced soon.

words.def: a deeply felt request for money, help or information that is needed immediately, especially one made by a charity or by the police
words.example: appeal for something, to launch a TV appeal for donations to the charity

words.def: a person who makes a formal request for something (= applies for it), especially for a job, a place at a college or university, etc.
words.example: There were over 500 applicants for the job.

words.def: a way of dealing with somebody/something; a way of doing or thinking about something such as a problem or a task
words.example: She took the wrong approach in her dealings with them.

words.def: suitable, acceptable or correct for the particular circumstances
words.example: an appropriate response/measure/method

words.def: in a way that is suitable, acceptable or correct for the particular circumstances
words.example: The government has been accused of not responding appropriately to the needs of the homeless.

words.def: the feeling that somebody/something is good or acceptable; a positive opinion of somebody/something
words.example: She desperately wanted to win her father's approval.

words.def: to think that somebody/something is good, acceptable or suitable
words.example: I told my mother I wanted to leave school but she didn't approve.

words.def: to happen; to start to exist
words.example: An opportunity arose to work in the United States.

words.def: involving the use of weapons
words.example: an armed robbery

words.def: weapons, especially as used by the army, navy, etc.
words.example: arms and ammunition

words.def: a thin stick with a sharp point at one end, which is shot from a bow
words.example: a bow and arrow

words.def: made or produced to copy something natural; not real
words.example: an artificial limb/flower/sweetener/fertilizer

words.def: connected with art or artists
words.example: the artistic works of the period

words.def: pictures and photographs prepared for books, magazines, etc.
words.example: Can you let me have the finished artwork for the poster by Friday?

words.def: feeling shame or feeling embarrassed about somebody/something or because of something you have done
words.example: ashamed of something, She was deeply ashamed of her behaviour at the party.

words.def: to one side; out of the way
words.example: She pulled the curtain aside.

words.def: a particular part or feature of a situation, an idea, a problem, etc.; a way in which it may be considered
words.example: aspect of something, The book aims to cover all aspects of city life.

words.def: to make a judgement about the nature or quality of somebody/something
words.example: assess somebody/something, Accurately assessing environmental impacts is very complex.

words.def: an opinion or a judgement about somebody/something that has been thought about very carefully
words.example: a detailed assessment of the risks involved

words.def: a person or thing that is valuable or useful to somebody/something
words.example: In his job, patience is an invaluable asset.

words.def: to give somebody something that they can use, or some work or responsibility
words.example: assign something (to somebody), The teacher assigned a different task to each of the children.

words.def: help or support
words.example: technical/economic/military assistance

words.def: to make a connection between people or things in your mind
words.example: associate somebody/something with somebody/something, I always associate the smell of baking with my childhood.

words.def: if one thing is associated with another, the two things are connected because they happen together or one thing causes the other
words.example: associated with doing something, the risks associated with taking drugs

words.def: an official group of people who have joined together for a particular purpose
words.example: Do you belong to any professional or trade associations?

words.def: to think or accept that something is true but without having proof of it
words.example: assume (that)…, It is reasonable to assume (that) the economy will continue to improve.

words.def: a belief or feeling that something is true or that something will happen, although there is no proof
words.example: an underlying/implicit assumption

words.def: to tell somebody that something is definitely true or is definitely going to happen, especially when they have doubts about it
words.example: assure somebody (that)…, You think I did it deliberately, but I assure you (that) I did not.

words.def: very surprising; difficult to believe
words.example: She ran 100m in an astonishing 10.6 seconds.

words.def: a document that you send to somebody using email
words.example: No attachment was included.

words.def: an act of trying to do something, especially something difficult, often with no success
words.example: I passed my driving test at the first attempt.

words.def: a public sale in which things are sold to the person who offers the most money for them
words.example: an auction of paintings

words.def: connected with sound that is recorded
words.example: audio and video recordings/files/clips

words.def: having controls that work without needing a person to operate them
words.example: automatic doors

words.def: without needing a person to operate controls
words.example: The heating switches off automatically.

words.def: knowing something; knowing that something exists and is important
words.example: awareness of something, an awareness of the importance of eating a healthy diet

words.def: making you feel embarrassed
words.example: There was an awkward silence.

words.def: located behind or at the back of something
words.example: We were sitting in the back row

words.def: towards a place or position that is behind
words.example: I lost my balance and fell backwards.

words.def: the simplest and smallest forms of life. Bacteria exist in large numbers in air, water and soil, and also in living and dead creatures and plants, and are often a cause of disease.
words.example: Neither chilling nor freezing kills all bacteria.

words.def: a small piece of metal or plastic, with a design or words on it, that a person wears or carries to show that they belong to an organization, support something, have achieved something, have a particular rank, etc.
words.example: She wore a badge saying ‘Vote for Coates’.

words.def: keeping or showing a balance so that different things or different parts of something exist in equal or correct amounts
words.example: The programme presented a balanced view of the two sides of the conflict.

words.def: a style of dancing that tells a dramatic story with music but no talking or singing
words.example: She wants to be a ballet dancer.

words.def: a small bag made of very thin rubber that becomes larger and rounder when you fill it with air or gas. Balloons are brightly coloured and used as decorations or toys.
words.example: to blow up/burst/pop a balloon

words.def: an official rule that says that something is not allowed
words.example: a smoking ban

words.def: a place where you can buy and drink alcoholic and other drinks
words.example: We arranged to meet in a bar called the Flamingo.

words.def: in a way that is just possible but only with difficulty
words.example: He could barely read and write.

words.def: a thing bought for less than the usual price
words.example: I picked up a few good bargains in the sale.

words.def: an object like a fence that prevents people from moving forward from one place to another
words.example: The crowd had to stand behind barriers.

words.def: a room or rooms in a building, partly or completely below the level of the ground
words.example: Kitchen goods are sold in the basement.

words.def: in the most important ways, without considering things that are less important
words.example: I think we are basically saying the same thing.

words.def: a container for holding or carrying things. Baskets are made of long thin pieces of material that bends and twists easily, for example plastic, wire or wicker.
words.example: a shopping basket

words.def: a fight between armies, ships or planes, especially during a war; a violent fight between groups of people
words.example: Napoleon was defeated at the Battle of Waterloo.

words.def: a heavy wild animal with thick fur and sharp claws (= pointed parts on the ends of its feet). There are many types of bear.
words.example: a black bear

words.def: a single hit to something, such as a drum, or a movement of something, such as your heart; the sound that this makes
words.example: several loud beats on the drum

words.def: to ask somebody for something especially in an anxious way because you want or need it very much
words.example: Now you have to beg and plead.

words.def: existence
words.example: The Irish Free State came into being in 1922.

words.def: a curve or turn, especially in a road or river
words.example: a sharp bend in the road

words.def: improving a situation; having a helpful or useful effect
words.example: A good diet is beneficial to health.
